Sep 27, 2005 CONTENTS: OpenJade OpenJade on Instructional computers Bug Reports OpenJade -------- Jade is James Clark's implementation of DSSSL -- Document Style Semantics and Specification Language -- an ISO standard for format

ecification Language -- an ISO standard for formatting SGML (and XML) documents (http://www.jclark.com/jade/). OpenJade is a project undertaken by the DSSSL community to maintain and extend Jade, as well as the related SP suite of SGML/XML p

ade, as well as the related SP suite of SGML/XML processing tools. OpenJade and OpenSP are distributed under the same license as Jade. See SourceForge (http://openjade.sourceforge.net/) for documentation and downloads. [This is not the sam

mentation and downloads. [This is not the same as the JADE platform for large transaction processing software systems, from Jade Software Corp (http://www.jadeworld.com)] OpenJade on Instructional computers ---------------------------------

puters. Bug Reports ----------- Please report problems with software on the Instructional systems to inst@eecs.berkeley.edu. Instructional Support Group 378/386/384 Cory, 333 Soda inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
